LLMProviderConfig.description:
LLM provider configuration — unified data contract.

How config is persisted varies by project:
- **DB-backed** (platform-orchestrator): provider_configs table, Fernet encryption
- **YAML** (prompt-engine): config.yaml with ${ENV_VAR} resolution
- **JSON** (auto-exec-mechanism): model_routes.json overrides
- **env** (platform-orchestrator/config.py): pydantic-settings BaseSettings

This model is the *in-memory representation* shared across all persistence
strategies. API key is in plaintext by design — encryption is the responsibility
of the storage layer (see orchestrator's Fernet helpers).

provider_type.description: Provider protocol type — determines which SDK/client to use
provider_type.title: Provider Type

base_url.description: API endpoint base URL (empty = use SDK default)
base_url.title: Base Url

api_key.description: API key (plaintext in this model — encrypt at rest)
api_key.title: Api Key

models.description: All available model names for this provider
models.title: Models

default_model.description: Default model used when not explicitly specified
default_model.title: Default Model

priority.description: Higher = preferred when multiple providers are equivalent
priority.title: Priority

min_tier.description: Minimum user access tier (0 = admin, 1 = all, 2+ = premium)
min_tier.title: Min Tier

id.description: DB record UUID (set by storage layer)
id.title: Id

LLMProviderConfig.title: LLMProviderConfig

ModelRoute.description:
Category-based model routing rule.

Maps a semantic task category to a (provider, model) pair.
Inspired by auto-exec-mechanism's routing table and oh-my-openagent patterns.

Example:
    route = ModelRoute(
        category="creative",
        provider="openai",
        model="gpt-4o",
        fallback_model="gpt-4o-mini",
        keywords=["write", "draft", "copy", "写作", "文案"],
        description="Creative writing / copy / content generation",
        cost_level="medium",
    )

model.description: Target model to use for this category
model.title: Model

fallback_model.description: Fallback model when primary is unavailable
fallback_model.title: Fallback Model

keywords.description: Keywords used by the classifier to auto-assign categories
keywords.title: Keywords

description.description: Human-readable description of what this category handles
description.title: Description

usage_count.description: How many times this route was hit
usage_count.title: Usage Count

ModelRoute.title: ModelRoute

UserLLMOverride.description:
Per-user provider override.

Allows individual users to supply their own API keys instead of using the
admin-configured shared key. This is the data contract for the orchestrator's
user_api_keys table.

When a user override exists, the system uses the user's key + optional base_url
instead of the admin config for that provider.

api_key.description: User's own API key (plaintext — encrypt at rest)
api_key.title: Api Key

base_url.description: Optional custom endpoint override for this user
base_url.title: Base Url

model.description: Optional model restriction for this user
model.title: Model

UserLLMOverride.title: UserLLMOverride

description:
Complete LLM configuration for a project/service.

Aggregates provider definitions, routing rules, and defaults into one object.
This is the top-level config that projects like platform-orchestrator can
construct from their storage layer and pass to consumers.

Example YAML-equivalent:
    providers:
      - name: openai
        provider_type: openai_compat
        base_url: https://api.openai.com/v1
        models: [gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini]
        default_model: gpt-4o
      - name: doubao
        provider_type: doubao
        ...

    routing:
      quick:
        provider: openai
        model: gpt-4o-mini
        cost_level: low
      deep:
        provider: openai
        model: gpt-4o
        cost_level: medium

    defaults:
      temperature: 0.7
      max_tokens: 4096
      timeout: 60
title: LLMGlobalConfig
